message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): Command::output_with_cx pre-spawn cancel gate (tick 430)

Pins the pre-spawn cancel gate on `Command::output_with_cx`. The
primitive has TWO cancel-observability points:

1. Pre-spawn `cx.checkpoint()` → Err::Interrupted with "pre-spawn" in
   the message, before any child process is forked.
2. Mid-flight via a cx→Arc<AtomicBool> watcher bridging caller-cx
   cancellation into the run_output_command worker's cancel flag
   (polled at PROCESS_POLL_INTERVAL=10ms).

This test pins (1). (2) already had a test (pre-existing from
ft-xbnl0.2.3 era:
`process_command_output_with_cx_cancellation_surfaces_as_interrupted`).
Together they now pin both observability points explicitly.

Test shape (tick 430, pre-spawn):
- Build `process::Command::new("sleep").arg("30")` (never spawned)
- Pre-cancel cx via `cx.cancel_with(User, ...)`
- Wrap `cmd.output_with_cx(&cx)` in 2 s outer safety-net
- Assert elapsed < 500 ms AND Err with ErrorKind::Interrupted and
  message containing "pre-spawn"

Pinning the pre-spawn gate specifically guards against a subtle
regression where a cancelled cx still spawns and then immediately
kills the child — observable as wasted forks, zombie handles, or
audit-log churn, none of which the mid-flight test catches.

Observed locally: test returns in under 20 ms (no fork involved).

Check script Run 6/6 filter extends to include `command_output_with_cx`
which picks up both my new pre-spawn test AND the pre-existing
mid-flight test (bonus coverage, filter-broadening pattern same as
tick-357 TLS and tick-427 Semaphore). Run 6 grows 14 → 16 tests.

Test counts:

  1/6 HTTP client       : 34
  2/6 TLS               : 45
  3/6 Regression guards :  3
  4/6 Metrics server    :  3
  5/6 Web server        :  2
  6/6 runtime_compat    : 16  (+1 new pre-spawn, +1 pre-existing mid-flight)
  total                 : 103

Full check script passes locally via fork bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -31,6 +31,13 @@
 #      The semaphore filter is `semaphore_acquire_` so it catches both
 #      borrow and owned variants.
 #      unix::next_line_with_cx pre-cancel test (tick 429).
+#      Command::output_with_cx pre-spawn pre-cancel test (tick 430).
+#      The command_output_with_cx filter also picks up the pre-existing
+#      mid-flight cancel test
+#      (process_command_output_with_cx_cancellation_surfaces_as_interrupted)
+#      as bonus coverage — together they pin both cancel-observability
+#      points on Command::output_with_cx (pre-spawn gate + mid-flight
+#      cx→AtomicBool watcher).
 #
 # Exit code 0 on all passing; non-zero on any failure.
 #
@@ -134,7 +141,7 @@ run_test "Run 6/6: runtime_compat primitive contracts (budget + cancel observati
     -p frankenterm-core \
     --features asupersync-runtime \
     --lib \
-    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx semaphore_acquire_ mpsc_recv_with_cx watch_changed_with_cx join_set_join_next_with_cx unix_next_line_with_cx
+    -- --nocapture _with_cx_observes_budget_deadline yield_now_with_cx oneshot_recv_with_cx broadcast_recv_with_cx semaphore_acquire_ mpsc_recv_with_cx watch_changed_with_cx join_set_join_next_with_cx unix_next_line_with_cx command_output_with_cx
 
 echo
 echo "=============================================================="
